titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Buswell, Photographs of Abandoned Wooden Buildings and Artifacts in Montana
Buswell (Richard), Photographs of Abandoned Wooden Buildings and Artifacts in Montana
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 11, 2018 at 19:02

did.unittitle: Photographs of Abandoned Wooden Buildings and Artifacts in Montana

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photographs created by Richard S. Buswell of Helena, Montana, of abandoned wooden buildings and artifacts in Montana, 1975-2016, and printed by him, 1986-2017. Images include exteriors and interiors of cabins, barns, hotels, general stores, and blacksmith shops, as well as railroad trestles and cemeteries.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Richard S. Buswell on the Arthur Corbitt Hoskins Memorial Fund, 2007-2015.
Purchased from Richard S. Buswell on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2013-2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized chronologically under headings for years between 1975 and 2016 and then alphabetically under each heading.

bioghist.head: Richard Buswell (born 1945)
bioghist.p: Richard Buswell is a medical doctor and photographer living in Helena, Montana.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard S. Buswell, Photographs of Abandoned Wooden Buildings and Artifacts in Montana. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Each folder in the collection contains a single photographic print.

scopecontent.head: Description of Papers
scopecontent.p: Photographs created by Richard Buswell of Helena, Montana, of abandoned wooden buildings and artifacts in Montana, 1975-2015, and printed by him, 1986-2016. Images include exteriors and interiors of cabins, barns, hotels, general stores, and blacksmith shops, as well as railroad trestles and cemeteries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs created by Richard Buswell of Helena, Montana, of abandoned wooden buildings and artifacts in Montana, 1975-2016, and printed by him, 1986-2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized chronologically under headings for years between 1975 and 2016 and then alphabetically under each heading.

did.unittitle: Loading Ramp and Mine Dump , Summer 1978
Aspen in a Cabin Door , Summer 1979
Great Northern Tunnel No. 1 , Late Winter 1993
Great Northern Tunnel No. 2 , Late Winter 1993
Hoe in a Lime Bin , Summer 1994
Seed Pods in a Corral , Summer 2008
